trajectory_summary: The call opened with a guarded but cooperative customer who had already submitted a form, had two competing quotes, and was motivated by bill pain — a qualified lead. The trajectory was damaged at 01:10 when LaFalla issued a channel-redirect directive and the rep responded with immediate counter-pressure rather than acknowledgment. The rep never recovered, and the call ended with a mutual hang-up at 02:33 with no appointment, no callback scheduled, and no door left open.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is listed as 'unknown.' Based on the transcript, the correct tag is 'no appointment / call abandoned.' LaFalla was not disqualified — he is a homeowner, has bill pain, has competitive context, and expressed interest in a written deliverable. The call ended due to rep handling failure, not customer disqualification.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: Don't bother me anymore on the phone. Send me an email or send a hard copy of how your company can help me with my, electric bill.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Bucky counter-pushed a channel-redirect directive without a single word of acknowledgment, which converted a guarded-but-cooperative customer into an adversarial one within seconds. The pattern repeated at 01:40 with the same result. The coaching move is specific: acknowledge the preference first ('Totally fair — I won't keep you on the phone'), then reframe the appointment as the mechanism that produces the written deliverable LaFalla actually wants. This sequence holds the send_proposal_before_meeting hard rule while keeping the door open. Until Bucky can execute the acknowledge-then-reframe in sequence, he will lose this customer type on the first redirect.

key_moments.quote: Sometimes it just it's a little it doesn't stick.
key_moments.stewart_read: LaFalla's mild frustration at 01:00 ('Didn't I put that on the phone?') was the earliest recoverable friction point in the call. The rep's vague deflection — 'it doesn't stick' — is the kind of non-answer that reads as evasive to a skeptical customer. The codex's honest-specificity principle applies here: a brief, direct acknowledgment ('The form captures the basics, but I need to confirm a couple of things live to make sure the design is accurate for your home') would have validated LaFalla's frustration and given him a reason for the call. Instead, the vague deflection primed the harder channel-redirect that followed 7 seconds later.

key_moments.quote: Don't bother me anymore on the phone. Send me an email or send a hard copy of how your company can help me with my, electric bill.
key_moments.stewart_read: LaFalla's channel-redirect is a contact-preference complaint, not a cost or interest objection. The correct move is to acknowledge the preference, then reframe: the appointment IS the written deliverable he wants — it produces a custom design with real numbers he can compare against his two existing quotes. The rep instead counter-pushed immediately ('Yeah. I can help you right now'), which read as dismissive of a stated preference. Note: the codex flags protocols.excessive_contact_complaint as TBD — this call is a clean source-of-truth candidate for populating that section.

key_moments.quote: Oh, so you haven't gotten enough information about me to to get to send you a quote? I want to send you a quote because I already have quotes from two other companies.
key_moments.stewart_read: LaFalla reveals he has two competing quotes and is actively seeking a third — this is a live competitive opening, not a disqualifier. The codex's already_saw_design rebuttal applies: 'It's always best to get a couple looks so you have something to compare against. We offer different equipment than most companies and our price points can be different.' The rep did not deploy this or any variant. Instead, the rep was already in a defensive posture from the channel-redirect exchange and had no bandwidth to recognize the competitive signal as an opportunity.

key_moments.quote: I want you to send me an email. List all of the ways that your company can help me lower my electricity bill or send me a hard copy in the mail.
key_moments.stewart_read: LaFalla's second channel-redirect is more specific — he wants a list of ways Ion can help, delivered in writing. The rep's response ('everything we do is specific to homes') is procedurally accurate but lands as a refusal without an alternative. The send_proposal_before_meeting hard rule prohibits sending the proposal pre-appointment, but it does not prohibit acknowledging the preference and bridging to the appointment as the mechanism that produces the written output. The rep treated the hard rule as a conversation-ender rather than a constraint to work within.
